# [clang] Assertion `isa<To>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"` failed

**Author:** Rush10233
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/149280
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** duplicate, clang:frontend, confirmed, crash, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-07-17T16:22:59Z

## Body

Goes back to clang-6: https://godbolt.org/z/ss7hKK4YK

Generated by fuzzer that randomly adds `consteval` specifier to functions.

Simplified Test:

```c++
template<typename T>
consteval int foo(int i) {
    T instance;
    return i;
}

using func_type = int(int);
func_type* p = __builtin_addressof(foo);
```

Stack Dump:

```
clang++: /root/llvm-project/llvm/include/llvm/Support/Casting.h:578: decltype(auto) llvm::cast(From*) [with To = clang::OverloadExpr; From = clang::Expr]: Assertion `isa<To>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.
Stack dump:
```

### Comment 2 - shafik

Please try and look for duplicates before submitting bug reports. We should not open new reports if we have an existing report. Although if the code is different enough adding the example to the existing bug is welcome.
